creator: sunpoet@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Oct 07, 2011 at 06:00:33PM +0000, Jean-Francois Dockes wrote:
>  There is already a patch in the database for an update to 1.2.3, it is
>  almost one year old.
>  
>  The migration of xapian to version 1.2 is waiting for a so-called repocopy
>  from xapian to xapian10 (the reason for wanting to keep xapian10 so long is
>  unknown to me).
>  
>  Henrik Brix Andersen <brix@FreeBSD.org> has as far as I know prepared a
>  patch to make the repocopy happen. Last I heard about it was june 2011.
>  
>  I already have a patch ready for an update to 1.2.7 (once the repository
>  situation is resolved), so the one in this PR is redundant, sorry.
>  
>  J.F. Dockes

Hi,

brix@ is cc'd.

What I know about the current status is:
1) 4 PRs in the database for xapian 1.2.3 but no one takes them currently.
2) Repocopy was abandoned.

That's why I submit these PRs for updating xapian ports to latest version.
Personally, I think it's OK to update xapian from 1.0.x to 1.2.x without
keeping the old versions. PORTREVISION bump is enough for those ports
depend on xapian ports. Regardless of the status of old PRs, if you agree
with the update (1.0.x to 1.2.7), we (brix, me or other committers) would
do the update right now.
Thanks.

Regards,
sunpoet
